segments.caption: The video opens with a vibrant Gucci advertisement featuring a model in a bold blue and red ensemble, accessorized with a chain-strap bag, set against a tropical backdrop with the 'GUCCI' logo prominently displayed. The scene transitions to a black-and-white archival clip of a woman in a striped dress dancing energetically in a sunlit outdoor setting, followed by a close-up of a woman in a dark top with a serious expression. The narrative shifts to a fashion show where models in avant-garde outfits, including one with a blue furry mask and another in a white hooded outfit with oversized sunglasses, walk past an audience. A close-up of a woman in a brown leather jacket appears, followed by a black-and-white scene of a woman in a white dress walking through a crowded, ornate space. The video then cuts to a woman in a bright green jacket walking in a dark environment, followed by a woman in a black jacket sitting at a table in a well-lit room with a window behind her. The scene changes to a nighttime cityscape with silhouettes of people on a rooftop overlooking a river, then to a magazine cover titled 'STYLE WARS' featuring two men in suits. A close-up of an older man with gray hair and a beard appears, followed by a man in a suit walking down a runway as the audience applauds. The video returns to the older man, now in a different setting, and then to a woman in a floral dress with dark nail polish, touching her hair. The final frames show a Gucci advertisement with a model in a blue top and red skirt, followed by a close-up of a woman in a patterned outfit with colorful beads, and ends with the woman in the black jacket at the table, smiling slightly.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman's eye, overlaid with a social media interface, transitioning to a model in a yellow hoodie with 'NY' printed on it, standing in front of an ornate door. The scene shifts to a man in a blue Gucci tracksuit with a crocodile-skin handbag, followed by a woman in a black zippered jacket seated at a table, gesturing as she speaks. The narrative then moves to a close-up of a Gucci-branded object, followed by a book being flipped open to reveal vintage Gucci imagery, including a black-and-white photo of a store and a colorful fashion spread. An older man with gray hair and tortoiseshell glasses appears, speaking thoughtfully, with archival photos of men in suits and a woman in a white dress overlaid. A text overlay quotes Madonna at the MTV Video Music Awards, emphasizing Gucci's cultural impact. The video transitions to a title card for 'Bloomberg Businessweek Presents Storylines: The Real House of Gucci,' followed by a scenic shot of a river in Florence with a waterfall and historic buildings. The final scene features a woman with short red hair and glasses, identified as Sara Forden, Editor of Bloomberg News and author of 'The House of Gucci,' speaking expressively in a well-lit room with a fireplace and bookshelf behind her.

segments.caption: The video opens with Sara Forden, a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated in a well-lit room with a fireplace and bookshelf behind her. She speaks expressively, using hand gestures to emphasize her points. The scene transitions to a black-and-white archival photo of Guccio Gucci, labeled with his name, followed by a timeline graphic indicating 'Gucci opens 1921'. The narrative continues with a family tree-style visual, introducing Aldo Gucci and Rodolfo Gucci through black-and-white portraits, connected by lines to Guccio Gucci. A group photo shows an older man, presumably Aldo Gucci, flanked by two women, set against a floral backdrop. The video then cuts to a vintage street scene of a Gucci store on Fifth Ave., New York, with pedestrians and a hot dog cart visible. Returning to Sara Forden, she continues her commentary. The family tree expands to include Geogio Gucci, Paolo Gucci, and Roberto Gucci, followed by Rodolfo Gucci’s son, Maurizio Gucci, whose portrait appears with a smile and glasses. The visual style remains consistent with black-and-white imagery and a dark background patterned with Gucci’s interlocking G logo, emphasizing the brand’s legacy and family dynamics.

segments.caption: The segment begins with Sara Forden, a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated in a well-lit room with a fireplace and bookshelf behind her. She speaks expressively, gesturing with her hands, which have red-painted nails and a silver watch on her left wrist. The scene transitions to a vintage color photograph of four men in tuxedos, including Maurizio Gucci, standing together at a formal event. This is followed by a photo of Maurizio Gucci and Patrizia Reggiani, both elegantly dressed, smiling at the camera against a jungle-themed backdrop. A black-and-white family tree graphic then appears, showing portraits of Maurizio Gucci and Patrizia Reggiani, with text indicating their split in 1985. The graphic updates to show Patrizia Reggiani’s name change to Patrizia Gucci, and a close-up archival clip from 1986 shows her in a brown leather jacket with dramatic makeup. The segment continues with a photo of Paolo Gucci in a suit standing beside a classical statue, followed by a photo of Patrizia Gucci and Paolo Gucci at a social event, and concludes with a black-and-white portrait of an older man with glasses, identified as Aldo Gucci, smiling thoughtfully with his hand on his chin.

segments.caption: The video opens with a black-and-white archival image of Aldo Gucci, smiling and wearing glasses, with his hand on his chin, set against an ornate background. The scene transitions to Sara Forden, a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated in a well-lit room with a fireplace and bookshelf behind her, speaking expressively with red-painted nails. A newspaper headline from the Los Angeles Times dated September 12, 1986, appears, reading 'Tearful Aldo Gucci Gets Prison Term in Tax Case,' with 'Aldo Gucci' highlighted in green. The video then cuts to a modern interview setting where Sara Forden, now in a black leather jacket, sits across from Domenico De Sole, an older man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit and light blue shirt, in a bright, minimalist room with large windows and bookshelves. They are engaged in conversation, with Sara holding papers. Close-ups show Domenico speaking thoughtfully, then smiling warmly. A graphic appears with a black-and-white photo of Aldo Gucci labeled 'Aldo Gucci' and text 'Aldo goes to jail — 1986,' set against a dark background with a repeating Gucci logo pattern. The interview continues with alternating close-ups of Sara and Domenico. The segment ends with a retro-style animated globe with glowing orange Chinese characters '日本通運' (Nihon Tsūun, meaning 'Japan Transport') rotating, followed by a blurry night scene of a Japanese cityscape with neon signs, suggesting a shift in narrative focus to Japan.

segments.caption: The video opens with a blurry, dark night scene of a Japanese cityscape, filled with glowing neon signs and vertical lights, evoking a bustling urban atmosphere. This transitions into a vibrant, sunlit daytime street scene in Japan, crowded with pedestrians walking past colorful storefronts and towering signs with Japanese characters, including one for 'Inc.' The scene then cuts to a close-up of a woman in a light pink blouse and green jacket, smiling warmly as she walks through the crowd. The focus shifts to an interview setting where Domenico De Sole, an older man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit and light blue shirt, speaks thoughtfully in a modern, well-lit room with large windows and bookshelves. He is seated across from Sara Forden, a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black leather jacket, who holds papers and listens attentively. The video then transitions to a panoramic view of Florence, showcasing its historic architecture, the Arno River, and the iconic Duomo under a clear sky. A close-up follows of a pigeon perched on a weathered building ledge above a window with ornate iron bars. The scene changes to a cobblestone street in Florence where people walk past outdoor market stalls displaying leather goods, including handbags and belts. A woman in a black jacket browses items, while another woman in a blue jacket examines a handbag. The camera pans through a narrow market alley lined with colorful handbags and accessories, capturing the lively atmosphere. The segment ends with a close-up of a row of luxury handbags in various colors and textures, including crocodile and snake skin, displayed on a wooden shelf, emphasizing craftsmanship and design.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman with shoulder-length light brown hair, wearing a black zippered jacket, browsing a display of colorful leather handbags at an outdoor market in Florence, her attention focused on the goods. The scene transitions to a close-up of her face as she speaks, then cuts to a woman wearing a red patterned headscarf, viewed from behind, walking past a Gucci store window displaying mannequins in stylish outfits. The camera then shows a wide shot of the Gucci store on a city street, with pedestrians walking by and a woman taking a photo of a group posing outside. The scene shifts to an interview setting where Rita Clifton, a brand expert and former chairman of Interbrand UK, speaks directly to the camera, gesturing with her hands. The video then cuts to archival black-and-white footage of two men in suits, one with a beard and the other with glasses, standing together at an event. This is followed by a close-up of a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black leather jacket, speaking in a modern, minimalist room. The video then displays a graphic with two black-and-white portraits: one labeled 'Maurizio Gucci' and the other 'Andrea Morante', with text identifying the latter as a Morgan Stanley investment banker. A color photograph of Andrea Morante, a man with a mustache in a suit, is shown seated in front of a cityscape. The final scene features Andrea Morante, now an older man with white hair, speaking animatedly in a formal interview setting, gesturing with his hands as he talks.

segments.caption: The video opens with a vintage color photograph of a group of five people—two women and three men—posing together in a formal indoor setting, likely from the 1980s, with one man in a light suit and another in a dark suit with a mustache. The scene transitions to a close-up of an older man with graying hair, wearing a dark suit and light shirt, speaking thoughtfully in a softly lit interview setting. This is followed by a black-and-white family tree graphic set against a dark background with a repeating Gucci logo pattern, showing Aldo Gucci at the top, with lines connecting to his three sons: Georgio Gucci, Paolo Gucci, and Roberto Gucci. Each son’s portrait is labeled with their name and share percentage: Georgio and Roberto each with 23.3%, and Paolo with 3.3%. The focus then shifts to Paolo Gucci’s portrait, where his share is replaced with a monetary value of $40,000,000. The narrative continues with a new graphic showing Paolo Gucci and Andrea Morante, with text indicating 'Paolo accepts offer to sell his shares,' followed by a visual of the Investcorp building, with text stating 'Paolo’s shares go to Investcorp.' The segment alternates between the interviewee’s expressive close-ups and the evolving graphics, emphasizing the financial and familial dynamics within the Gucci empire.

segments.caption: The video opens with archival footage from 1995, showing a tense scene outside a stone building with arched doorways, where a crowd of reporters, photographers, and police officers, including Carabinieri, gather around a white police vehicle with blue flashing lights. The atmosphere is chaotic, with cameras raised and people moving quickly. A body covered in a white sheet is carried on a stretcher by uniformed personnel, surrounded by onlookers and media. The scene shifts to an indoor interview setting, where a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, speaks expressively, gesturing with her hands. The setting is bright and cozy, with books, a lamp, and decorative items visible in the background. The video cuts back to the 1995 scene, showing the body being loaded into a gray van marked 'DOMENICO DE SOLE' and '438', with police and media still present. The woman in the interview continues speaking, her hands clasped or moving to emphasize her points. On-screen text identifies a police inspector, Paolo La Forgia, stating, 'He was shot three times in the head - like an execution,' adding a grim context to the archival footage. The sequence alternates between the somber, chaotic 1995 event and the calm, reflective interview, creating a contrast between past violence and present reflection.

segments.caption: The video begins with a woman with short red hair and glasses, wearing a black sweater over a white collared shirt, seated in a bright, cozy room with books and a lamp in the background, speaking expressively with hand gestures. The scene transitions to a vintage mugshot of a woman with dark, short hair, labeled 'POLIZIA SCIENTIFICA,' suggesting a police context. This is followed by exterior shots of a grand stone courthouse with large windows and a sign reading 'AULA IV CORTE DI ASSISE,' establishing a legal setting. Inside, a man in a suit and tie, identified as Gaetano Pecorella, Patrizia Reggiani’s lawyer, speaks in an interview setting. The video then cuts to archival footage from the Milan Courthouse in 1998, showing Patrizia Reggiani seated at a witness stand, wearing glasses and a dark blazer over a blue turtleneck, delivering a statement: 'I was naive to the point of stupidity. I found myself involved, but never complicit. This I deny now and I will deny it always.' The narrative continues with a black-and-white graphic timeline featuring a portrait of Patrizia Reggiani, labeled with her name, and text indicating she was sentenced to 29 years in prison in 1998. The final frames show a vintage color photograph of Patrizia Reggiani with Maurizio Gucci, both smiling, with Patrizia in a black sequined dress and large hoop earrings, and Maurizio in a tuxedo with a bow tie, set against a backdrop with a stylized leopard and foliage, evoking a glamorous 1980s social event.

segments.caption: The video opens with archival footage of a man in a dark suit confidently walking down a runway under a spotlight, flanked by an applauding audience, suggesting a high-profile fashion show. The scene transitions to a close-up of the Older man with gray hair and beard, speaking thoughtfully in a softly lit room with a framed picture in the background, his expression serious and reflective. The narrative shifts to a modern retail setting, showing a light blue double-breasted suit on a mannequin in a Gucci store window, with pedestrians passing by, followed by a shot of the Gucci store entrance with its iconic signage. The camera then moves to a Chanel storefront, where people wearing masks are seen entering, indicating a contemporary urban setting. A brief shot captures a man in a studded black jacket walking with a woman in a hat, adding a street-style element. The scene cuts to the Woman with short red hair and glasses, seated in a bright, cozy room with books and a fireplace, speaking expressively with red-painted nails and gesturing with her hands. The video then returns to the Older man with gray hair and beard, continuing his interview, before transitioning to a fashion show with models in avant-garde outfits—first a model in a blue furry mask and red tassels, then another in a white hooded outfit with oversized white sunglasses—walking down a dark runway under dramatic lighting. The segment concludes with a model in a colorful, patterned jacket with musical note embellishments, walking confidently down the runway, emphasizing the bold and experimental nature of the fashion being showcased.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic fashion runway sequence, showcasing models in avant-garde Gucci ensembles under dramatic, dark lighting. A model in a colorful, floral-patterned coat with a bejeweled hood walks confidently, followed by another in a sheer black tank top and bright yellow underwear, emphasizing bold, provocative design. The scene transitions to a woman with shoulder-length light brown hair, wearing a black zippered jacket, seated in front of a window with a garden view, speaking as a brand expert—identified as Rita Clifton. The video then cuts back to the runway, featuring a model in a black velvet dress with a dramatic ruffled collar, followed by a model in a light blue Gucci tracksuit with red and white stripes, adorned with a red beaded necklace. A close-up of a model in a black leather jacket with red trim and a chain-strap bag appears, overlaid with an Instagram post from Gucci detailing the #GucciLoveParade campaign, featuring celebrities like Miley Cyrus and Snoop Dogg. The sequence continues with a model in a green leather dress walking down a blue-lit runway, followed by a model in a white, fringed gown with intricate silver embellishments. The video concludes with a black screen displaying the Bloomberg Businessweek credits, listing producers, editors, and contributors, with the Bloomberg Businessweek logo prominently featured.
